data.title: KWESST Micro Systems looks to raise C$3.5 million in private placement
data.description:
• KWESST Micro Systems (TSXV:KWE:CA) said it will issue and sell its shares to raise C$3.5 million in a private placement.
  • The firm expects to issue 3.79M shares and/or pre-funded warrants to acquire one share at a price of C$0.928 apiece.
  • Each share or pre-funded warrant is bundled with one common share purchase warrant, and each warrant is immediately exercisable and entitles the holder to acquire one share at an exercise price of C$1.16 apiece.
  • The firm expects to use the net proceeds for general working capital purposes.

data.title: BWX Technologies wins $2.1B in contracts for U.S. Naval nuclear reactor components
data.description:
BWX Technologies (NYSE:BWXT) +2.5% pre-market Wednesday after announcing ~$2.1B in contract awards for the manufacture of reactor components for the U.S. Naval Nuclear Propulsion Program.

BWXT (NYSE:BWXT) said nuclear reactor component manufacturing and material procurement activities for Columbia and Virginia class submarines, as well as Ford class aircraft carriers, will be performed at its operations sites in Virginia, Ohio and Indiana over seven years.

Additionally, production and delivery of fuel and support activities for the U.S. Naval Nuclear Propulsion Program are performed by Nuclear Fuel Services Inc. in Tennessee; the contract, valued at $300M, was awarded and announced in October 2023, with work scheduled for completion this year.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Analog Devices tops FQ1 estimates; initiates FQ2 outlook
Gibraltar reports mixed Q4 earnings; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Gibraltar press release (NASDAQ:ROCK): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.01 beats by $0.10.
  • Revenue of $302.1M (-8.1% Y/Y) misses by $5.83M.
  • Strong 2024 Cash Generation of $174 Million.
  • Expands Agtech’s Structures Business with Acquisition of Lane Supply.
  • 2025 Guidance: Net Sales $1.40B – $1.45B vs. $1.40B consensus, GAAP EPS $4.25 - $4.50, Adjusted EPS $4.80 - $5.05 vs. $4.74 consensus.

data.title: Plymouth Industrial REIT promotes Anthony Saladino to President and CFO
data.description:
• Plymouth Industrial REIT (NYSE:PLYM) said on Wednesday that it has promoted Anthony Saladino to President and Chief Financial Officer, effective immediately.
  • Saladino has served as Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer since February 2022, having previously held the roles of Chief Accounting Officer and Senior Vice President of Accounting.
  • Before joining Plymouth in October 2020, he gained extensive experience leading finance and accounting teams in publicly traded companies, including serving as Chief Accounting Officer for AFIN (now GNL) and NYC REIT from 2017 to 2019 and as Vice President of Finance for The Ryland Group (now Lennar) from 2004 to 2011.
  • Plymouth Industrial REIT (NYSE:PLYM) shares were up by 1.39% in Wednesday pre-market trading.

data.title: Glencore plunges in London on full-year profit drop; to review London listing
data.description:
Glencore (OTCPK:GLCNF) (OTCPK:GLNCY) -7% in London trading Wednesday after reporting lower full-year core earnings, even as it returned $2.2B to shareholders.

FY 2024 adjusted EBITDA fell 16% Y/Y to $14.36B, missing analyst expectations of $14.61B according to a consensus compiled by Visible Alpha and marking the second straight year of lower profit following two record years that were driven by soaring metals' prices.

Glencore (OTCPK:GLCNF) (OTCPK:GLNCY) said lower prices for thermal coal and gas materially dragged on its results but the market for copper and zinc concentrates was favorable.

The company said it plans to return ~$2.2B to shareholders, including a top-up buyback of $1B, despite booking impairments worth $5.3B in 2024, including at its South African coal operations; a year ago, it made no buyback announcement alongside its 2023 results as it used cash to pay for its $6.9B purchase of Elk Valley Resources.

The impairments caused Glencore (OTCPK:GLCNF) (OTCPK:GLNCY) to swing to a full-year $1.63B net loss from $4.28B in profit a year earlier, while full-year revenues rose 6% Y/Y to $230.9B, slightly below analyst consensus.

Glencore (OTCPK:GLCNF) (OTCPK:GLNCY) also said it is considering whether to move its primary listing from London, where the company has traded since 2011, in what was the LSE's largest ever IPO at the time.

"Ultimately, what we want to ensure is that our securities are traded on the right exchange where we can get the right and optimal valuation for our stock," CEO Gary Nagle said on the company's earnings conference call. "There have been questions raised previously around whether London is the right exchange."

Glencore's (OTCPK:GLCNF) (OTCPK:GLNCY) shares are down nearly 13% over the past year, and switching to the U.S. would be a positive catalyst, Jefferies analysts Christopher LaFemina and Patricia Hove say.

Glencore's (OTCPK:GLCNF) (OTCPK:GLNCY) $1B share buyback helps ease concerns over the slight miss to 2024 EBITDA, but the company is not expected to benefit from strong commodity tailwinds until next year, according to LaFemina and Hove.

data.title: TransUnion names Tiffani Chambers as COO
data.description:
• TransUnion (NYSE:TRU) said it has appointed Tiffani Chambers as its chief operations officer and executive vice president, effective February 19.
  • Chambers most recently served as the COO of the retail banking division of Bank of America.

data.title: Palantir Technologies enters into partnership with SAUR
data.description:
• Palantir Technologies (NASDAQ:PLTR) said it has entered into a multi-year strategic partnership with SAUR, to work on the contract management processes through Palantir Foundry’s advanced generative AI capabilities.
  • Under the partnership, SAUR will strengthen its contract management capabilities by leveraging Palantir Foundry and AI Platform.
  • "This partnership with Palantir allows us to transform contract management by turning vast and complex datasets into clear, actionable intelligence. It represents a major step in our AI-driven transformation, reinforcing our ability to enhance compliance, streamline operations, and better serve our customers,” said chief information officer of SAUR, Rony Bejjani.

data.title: M&A Snapshot: Kontoor Brands acquires Helly Hansen in $900M deal, expanding outdoor & workwear market
Bausch + Lomb beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Bausch + Lomb press release (NYSE:BLCO): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.25 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $1.28B (+9.4% Y/Y) beats by $30M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA excluding Acquired IPR&D (non-GAAP)1 of $259 Million.
  • Revenue Grew 9% as Reported and 11% on a Constant Currency1 Basis Compared to the Fourth Quarter of 2023, with Growth Across All Segments.
  • Cash flow from operations for the full year of 2024 was $232 million, as compared to cash flow used in operations of $17 million for the full year of 2023, an increase of $249 million.
  • Adjusted EBITDA excluding Acquired IPR&D (non-GAAP)1 was $259 million for the fourth quarter of 2024, as compared to $231 million for the fourth quarter of 2023, an increase of $28 million.

  • 2025 Financial Outlook:

    Bausch + Lomb provided guidance for the full year of 2025, as follows.

    As of February 19, 2025

    Full-Year Revenue

    $4.950B - $5.050B vs. $5.02B consensus vs. estimated growth of 5.35% Y/Y

    ~5.5 - 7.5% constant currency growth1

    Full-Year Adjusted EBITDA

    $900M - $950M

    Excluding Acquired IPR&D (non-GAAP)1

    Full-Year Revenue Foreign Exchange Headwinds

    -$100M

    Full-Year Adj. EBITDA1 Foreign Exchange Headwinds

    -$20M
